mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid spike in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points within 30 calendar days
failure_condition: Forward returns following unemployment spikes are not statistically negative compared to control periods, or the signal produces false breakouts in >40% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in housing starts and consumer credit outstanding confirm synchronized strength in durable goods demand and household balance sheets, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over 60-day windows, or the correlation between HOUST and RSAFS drops below 0.2 without affecting SPX direction.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production accelerates beyond its 12-month moving average during a late-cycle expansion, firms exercise pricing power and expand operating margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production acceleration consistently coincides with negative or flat 20-day equity returns, or the mechanism loses predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% annualized confirms robust aggregate demand expansion, directly increasing corporate revenue trajectories and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate exceeds 2.5% annualized
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.5% does not produce positive forward returns in >55% of observations, or the signal's information coefficient decays to zero within 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month payroll growth and a stable or declining unemployment rate validate labor market tightness without triggering wage-inflation spirals, supporting sustained consumer spending and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll growth and unemployment stability do not jointly predict positive 5-day returns, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in the consumer price index erodes real disposable income and forces restrictive monetary policy, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPI increases do not lead to negative 20-day forward returns, or equity multiples expand despite rising inflation, breaking the discount rate transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds 105 and real GDP growth remains positive, manufacturing efficiency gains outpace demand constraints, expanding corporate profit margins and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined INDPRO and GDPC1 signal yields a negative or zero Sharpe ratio over rolling 2-year periods, or forward returns are indistinguishable from random noise.
